text: The aim of this video is to show the different steps of sclerotherapy with polydocanol form in the treatment of second degree hemorrhoidal

The surgeon prepares polydocanol foam by mixing 1.6 milliliters of 3% liquid polydocanol with 7.4 milliliters of sterile air using the Easy Foam Kit syringe system, resulting in a visible foam mixture.
The surgeon re-emulsifies the polydocanol foam for twenty seconds before injecting two cc of foam into each hemorrhoidal pile.

The surgeon performs the procedure using a modified Blanc Blanchard technique, administering a tangential injection of polydocanol foam into the hemorrhoid. This is done with a self-lighting open-ended anoscope and a XXG needle.

We strongly recommend the injection of the foam above the dented line and with a tangential approach in order to avoid postoperative pain and prosthetic or urinary complication especially during the treatment of the eleven o'clock located pile in male patient.
The surgeon repeats the sclerotherapy procedure by injecting polydocanol foam into the remaining two hemorrhoidal piles using a self-lighting open-ended anoscope and XXG needle.
